name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
description: Adversaries use application access tokens (OAuth2 access tokens, JWT bearer tokens) to authenticate without requiring username and password credentials, bypassing MFA controls. In Azure/Entra ID, adversaries steal and replay access tokens obtained via AiTM phishing, token harvesting from compromised endpoints, or extraction from browser memory/caches. Unlike password-based authentication, token replay does not re-trigger MFA and can access any resource the token's scope permits. SigninLogs records token replay as a successful authentication with singleFactorAuthentication if the token was issued without MFA in the original session.
iocs: SigninLogs ResultType 0 with AuthenticationRequirement 'singleFactorAuthentication' for a user enrolled in MFA — MFA was not challenged, indicating token replay
SigninLogs IPAddress changes between the original authentication and a subsequent token refresh (AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs) — token moved to different host
SigninLogs ConditionalAccessStatus 'notApplied' combined with successful access to high-value resources by an MFA-required account
Rapid token issuance for multiple resources (Graph, Exchange, SharePoint) within seconds — automated token farming from stolen primary refresh token
AuditLogs 'Add OAuth2PermissionGrant' immediately followed by SigninLogs token issuance for a new app — malicious app consent then immediate use
SigninLogs UserAgent changing between the primary authentication and subsequent non-interactive refresh tokens — token extracted and replayed from different environment
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs showing Primary Refresh Token (PRT) redemption from a device not in the organization's Intune device inventory
evasion_variants: Replay access tokens from a residential proxy IP geographically close to the victim to avoid impossible-travel triggers
Use the stolen access token only for read operations on a single resource to minimize scope-based anomaly signals
Replay the PRT from a device that mimics the victim's device fingerprint (device OS, browser UserAgent) obtained from prior reconnaissance
Use the access token during the victim's normal working hours to blend with legitimate activity patterns in their timeline
Limit replay session duration to less than the token's natural expiry without performing a refresh — no refresh token events generated
Access resources via Microsoft-native clients (Outlook, Teams) using the stolen token rather than direct API calls — generates less anomalous UserAgent patterns
Use the stolen refresh token to generate new access tokens for different resources one at a time over an extended period
detection_kql_hints: SigninLogs | where ResultType == 0 and AuthenticationRequirement == 'singleFactorAuthentication' | where UserPrincipalName in (mfa_required_users) | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, AppDisplayName, ConditionalAccessStatus

AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs | where ResultType == 0 | extend OriginalIP = tostring(DeviceDetail.deviceId) | join kind=inner (SigninLogs | where ResultType == 0 | project UserPrincipalName, OriginalIPAddress=IPAddress, TimeGenerated) on UserPrincipalName | where IPAddress != OriginalIPAddress and abs(datetime_diff('hour', TimeGenerated, TimeGenerated1)) < 2
SigninLogs | where TokenIssuerType == 'AzureAD' and ConditionalAccessStatus == 'notApplied' | where UserPrincipalName in (ca_policy_required_users)
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs | summarize ResourceCount=dcount(ResourceDisplayName) by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m) | where ResourceCount > 5
SigninLogs | where AuthenticationDetails has 'previouslySatisfied' | where IPAddress != prev(IPAddress, 1) | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, AuthenticationDetails
